source_pr.title: Infoschema: Refactor charsets, collations
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

I noticed that SHOW CHARACTER SET and SHOW COLLATION commands differ from infoschema tables.  The infoschema tables were just hard-coded values, including incorrect default collations.

### What is changed and how it works?

The information_schema tables are now unified to present the same information as the `SHOW` commands, which come from `./util/charset/charset.go` instead of hard coding.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Side effects

 - Breaking backward compatibility

The main downside to this change is that `SELECT * FROM information_schema.collations` and `SELECT * FROM information_schema.COLLATION_CHARACTER_SET_APPLICABILITY` shows a lot more rows.  This includes charsets and collations which are not actually supported.  But on the plus side, at least it is consistent.

Existing tests pass, but please let me know if/where new integration tests should be added.

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("SELECT default_collate_name, maxlen FROM information_schema.character_sets ORDER BY character_set_name").Check( (infoschema/tables_test.go)

fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
